message:
fix(compile): cover new Error variants in error_renderer + retry (ft-h9g0q)

After 79702a50 ("ux(errors): type runtime, pane, and watchdog failures")
added three new variants to `crate::error::Error`:

  - RuntimeOperation { operation, source }
  - PaneOperation { pane_id, operation, source }
  - WatchdogWarningRead { backend, source }

…two match sites in the crate kept raising E0004 non-exhaustive match
against `&Error`:

  - crates/frankenterm-core/src/output/error_renderer.rs:34 (`error_code`)
  - crates/frankenterm-core/src/retry.rs:378 (`is_retryable`)

Workspace `cargo check` fails on HEAD because of these two sites (ft-h9g0q).
This patch adds the missing arms with conservative behaviour:

  error_code:
    RuntimeOperation | PaneOperation | WatchdogWarningRead → "FT-9001"
    (same bucket used by the deprecated Runtime variant; per-variant
     codes can be assigned later without touching this site)

  is_retryable:
    RuntimeOperation | PaneOperation | WatchdogWarningRead → true
    (mirrors the Runtime fallback; follow-up can inspect the inner
     `source` field and flip non-transient failures to false)

Comments at each site cite ft-h9g0q and 79702a50 so the typed-variant
owner can refine the mapping without having to rediscover the wiring.

No behaviour change for callers that never produce the new variants;
callers that do get the same treatment as `Error::Runtime` previously
did, which is the safe default until typed handling is wired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -76,6 +76,17 @@ impl ErrorRenderer {
             Error::Io(_) => "FT-9002",
             Error::Json(_) => "FT-9003",
             Error::Runtime(_) => "FT-9001",
+            // [ft-h9g0q] Typed runtime/pane/watchdog variants added in
+            // 79702a50 ("ux(errors): type runtime, pane, and watchdog
+            // failures"). Route them through the existing generic code
+            // bucket until per-variant codes are assigned in
+            // error_code.rs — keeps the CLI JSON shape consistent and
+            // unblocks the workspace check. FT-9001 is the same
+            // bucket the deprecated Runtime variant uses, so
+            // behaviourally identical for operators.
+            Error::RuntimeOperation { .. }
+            | Error::PaneOperation { .. }
+            | Error::WatchdogWarningRead { .. } => "FT-9001",
             Error::SetupError(_) => "FT-6001",
             Error::Cancelled(_) => "FT-9004",
             Error::Panicked(_) => "FT-9005",
